rulings.Case Title: RICHARD THEODORE LUKA VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 14, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Specially appearing defendant Power Equipment Company.¿s motion to quash service of summons is granted. Plaintiffs failed to satisfy their burden of demonstrating that Power Equipment Company had the requisite contacts with California in order to subject it to personal jurisdiction. Plaintiffs¿ request for a continuance to conduct jurisdictional discovery is denied. Plaintiffs made no showing that their proposed discovery might (much less, would) produce evidence of any contacts of Power Equipment Company with California. Moreover, the case has been pending for almost 28 months and there has been no explanation why plaintiffs are, at what should be near the conclusion of their case, requesting jurisdictional discovery based solely on a hope that some information may be uncovered to enable them to proceed against one of the defendants, which inevitably further delays the case as to all other remaining defendants. = (220/HEK)
